title: salvia whitehousei var. parviflora
intro: Salvia whitehousei var. parviflora, also known as the dwarf white sage, is a low-growing, shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States, particularly in the states of Arizona and New Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Salvia whitehousei var. parviflora include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and its leaves can also be used in smudging ceremonies for their purifying properties.
